title: 4-Phase Claude Code Workflow (Plan then Implement then Verify)
description: Boris Cherny recommended workflow: Explore (Plan Mode, no changes) then Plan (Plan Mode, review with Ctrl+G) then Implement (Normal/Auto) then Commit (with descriptive message).
workflow: 1. EXPLORE: Enter Plan Mode (Ctrl+G), read files, understand architecture, ask clarifying questions. No changes.
2. PLAN: Still in Plan Mode, create detailed implementation plan. Review with Ctrl+G. Get user approval.
3. IMPLEMENT: Exit Plan Mode. Execute the approved plan step by step. Run tests after each step.
4. VERIFY: Run full test suite. Take screenshots for UI changes. Verify with expected outputs.
5. COMMIT: git add specific files, git commit with descriptive message. Create PR if needed.
6. CLEAN UP: /clear between unrelated tasks. /compact at ~75% context usage.

title: CLAUDE.md Cascade Configuration
description: How to structure CLAUDE.md files across locations for maximum effectiveness.
workflow: 1. Create ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md with global preferences (under 50 lines): preferred style, common commands, language preferences.
2. Create ./CLAUDE.md at project root with project conventions (under 300 lines): Bash commands Claude cannot guess, code style that differs from defaults, test commands, architectural decisions.
3. Create ./CLAUDE.local.md for personal overrides (add to .gitignore): personal preferences, local environment quirks.
4. Add subdirectory CLAUDE.md files for module-specific context: test commands per subdirectory, module-specific conventions.
5. Review and prune CLAUDE.md every 3-6 months as model capabilities evolve. Delete rules Claude already follows by default.

title: Subagent Delegation Pattern
description: Pattern for using subagents to preserve main context window while performing research, review, or exploration tasks.
workflow: 1. Identify tasks that would bloat context: codebase exploration, code review, multi-file search.
2. Use Agent tool with appropriate subagent_type: code-explorer for searches, code-reviewer for reviews, architect for design.
3. Provide specific, scoped prompts to subagents. Be explicit about what to find and report back.
4. Subagent returns only a summary, keeping main context clean.
5. For parallel independent tasks, launch multiple agents in a single message.
6. Never delegate understanding. Always synthesize the subagent summary yourself before acting on it.

title: Hook System for Deterministic Automation
description: Configure hooks for automatic actions at key moments in Claude Code sessions.
workflow: 1. Create ~/.claude/settings.json hooks section.
2. Add PreToolUse hook for Bash: runs audit log of all shell commands.
3. Add PostToolUse hook for Edit|Write: runs auto-lint (ruff, prettier, gofmt).
4. Add SessionStart hook: heartbeat, environment check, MCP server readiness.
5. Add Stop hook: cleanup temp files, close processes, verify no stale artifacts.
6. Configure matcher patterns to target specific tools (Bash, Edit, Write).
7. Test hooks with /hooks command. Verify deterministic behavior.
8. Monitor hook output for false positives. Adjust matchers as needed.

title: Worktree Parallel Development Pattern
description: Use git worktrees to run parallel Claude Code sessions on different branches without context interference.
workflow: 1. Create worktree: claude --worktree feature-auth
2. This creates .claude/worktrees/feature-auth/ on a new branch.
3. Work on the feature in the worktree session with full isolation.
4. Main session continues on original branch, no interference.
5. When done: exit worktree (choose keep or remove).
6. Merge the feature branch: git merge feature-auth on main.
7. Writer/Reviewer pattern: one session writes, another reviews.
8. Fan-out: loop through files calling claude -p for batch operations.

title: CLAUDE.md Template for AI/ML Projects
description: Example CLAUDE.md file for a machine learning project with Docker, GPU, and model conventions.
code:
# AI/ML Project Conventions

## Environment
- Python 3.14, Node 26
- Docker Compose for services
- GPU: RTX 5090 24GB

## Commands
- Run tests: `pytest tests/ -v`
- Format: `ruff check --fix . && ruff format .`
- Type check: `mypy src/`
- Docker rebuild: `docker compose up -d --build`

## Code Style
- Use type hints on all function signatures
- Prefer dataclasses over dicts for structured data
- Use pathlib.Path instead of os.path
- Error handling: prefer specific exceptions over bare except

## Architecture
- MCP servers in /mcp/ directory
- Docker Compose in project root
- Models stored in /models/ (gitignored)
- Config in .env (gitignored)

title: Hook Configuration for Auto-Lint
description: Settings.json hooks configuration that automatically lints code after edits.

title: MCP Server Configuration for Claude Code
description: Example .claude.json MCP server configuration for a multi-tool AI stack.

title: Cost Optimization Configuration
description: Claude Code settings and practices to minimize token usage and cost.
code:
# Cost optimization practices for Claude Code

## Settings (in ~/.claude/settings.json)
{
  "permissions": {
    "defaultMode": "bypassPermissions"
  }
}

## Session practices:
# 1. Default to Sonnet for day-to-day work
# 2. Use Opus only for complex architecture decisions
# 3. Clear between tasks: /clear
# 4. Compact at ~75%: /compact
# 5. Write specific prompts (saves ~70% tokens vs vague)
# 6. Plan before implementing (cheaper than iterating bad code)
# 7. Disconnect unused MCP servers (each burns context tokens)
# 8. Keep CLAUDE.md under 2,000 tokens
# 9. Targeted file reads (specify line ranges)
# 10. Press Escape early on bad responses

title: Context Window Management Pattern
description: Strategies for managing Claude Code context window: /clear between unrelated tasks, /compact at ~75% usage, /btw for side questions, keep CLAUDE.md lean, use subagents for exploration.

title: Verification-Driven Development Pattern
description: The single highest-leverage practice: include tests, screenshots, or expected outputs so Claude can verify its own work. Instead of implement email validation, say write validateEmail. Test cases: user@example.com -> true, invalid -> false. Run the tests.

title: Anti-Patterns to Avoid
description: Common Claude Code anti-patterns: kitchen-sink sessions (mixing tasks), over-correcting (same issue, multiple attempts), over-specified CLAUDE.md (too many rules), trust-without-verify gap, infinite exploration (unscoped investigation), ghost context (assuming memory across sessions).

title: Model Selection Strategy
description: Sonnet 4.6 for day-to-day development. Opus 4.6 for complex architecture, multi-agent, and extended autonomous sessions. Haiku 4.5 for sub-agents, linting, and docs. Key insight: the most capable model is often cheaper because it completes tasks faster with less correction.

title: Multi-Agent Orchestration with Claude Code
description: Pattern for running multiple Claude Code sessions in parallel using worktrees or /loop. Writer/Reviewer: one session writes, another reviews. Fan-out: /loop for batch operations. Council: multiple sessions debate solutions.

title: Error Recovery and Debugging Pattern
description: How to recover from common Claude Code errors and debug failing sessions.
workflow: 1. Read the error message carefully. Claude Code usually includes the file path and line number.
2. If Edit fails: check that old_string matches exactly (including whitespace). Use Read first to verify.
3. If Bash fails: check the command syntax. Try running it manually first.
4. If context window is full: run /compact to summarize, then /clear if needed.
5. If MCP server fails: check server logs, restart with docker compose restart <service>.
6. If tool call is denied: check permissions in settings.json or CLAUDE.md.
7. For persistent issues: start a new session with /clear and re-approach the problem.
8. Escalate: use gh issue create to file a bug report with reproduction steps.

title: Git Integration Pattern
description: How to use Claude Code with git for version control, commits, and PRs.
workflow: 1. Before starting: check git status and current branch.
2. Work in feature branches: git checkout -b feature/description
3. Commit frequently: describe what changed and why in the message.
4. Use conventional commits: feat:, fix:, refactor:, docs:, test:
5. Before PR: run tests, lint, type check.
6. Create PR with gh pr create with descriptive title and body.
7. Review: use Claude Code to review PRs with gh pr view and gh api.
8. After merge: delete feature branch, pull main, start fresh.

title: MCP Server Development Pattern
description: Build a custom MCP server with FastMCP and integrate it with Claude Code.
workflow: 1. Create project: mkdir my-mcp && cd my-mcp
2. Install FastMCP: pip install mcp[cli]
3. Create server.py with FastMCP server and tools
4. Define tools with @mcp.tool() decorator
5. Add resources with @mcp.resource() for read-only data
6. Test locally: python server.py
7. Add to .claude.json mcpServers configuration
8. For Docker: create Dockerfile exposing SSE on a port
9. Add healthcheck for the container
10. Test integration: ask Claude Code to use your new tool

title: Custom MCP Server with FastMCP
description: Minimal MCP server with a tool and a resource using FastMCP Python SDK.

title: CLAUDE.md with Custom Commands
description: CLAUDE.md file with custom commands and project-specific conventions.
code:
# Project Conventions

## Build & Test
- Build: `docker compose build`
- Test: `pytest tests/ -v --cov=src`
- Lint: `ruff check --fix . && ruff format .`
- Type check: `mypy src/`

## Architecture
- Monorepo with /src/, /tests/, /mcp/ directories
- FastAPI for REST endpoints
- MCP servers on port 8xxx
- Docker Compose for all services

## Custom Commands
- `/review` - Run full code review on current changes
- `/deploy` - Build and push Docker images
- `/test-changed` - Run tests for changed files only
- `/check-health` - Verify all MCP servers are responding

## Git Workflow
- Feature branches from main
- Conventional commits: feat:, fix:, refactor:
- Squash merge PRs

## Do NOT
- Never commit .env files
- Never push to main directly
- Never skip tests before merging

title: Memory System for Cross-Session Context
description: Using Claude Code memory files to persist context across sessions.
code:
# Memory files are stored in ~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/
# They persist across sessions and are automatically loaded

# To save a memory, create a file:
# ~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/feedback_testing.md
---
name: feedback-testing
metadata:
  type: feedback
description: Testing preferences for this project
---
Always use pytest with -v flag for verbose output.
Prefer integration tests over unit tests for API endpoints.
Never mock the database - use test containers instead.

# MEMORY.md serves as an index:
- [Testing preferences](feedback_testing.md) - Always pytest -v, integration over unit
- [Architecture decisions](project_arch.md) - FastAPI, MCP on 8xxx ports

title: Claude Code Memory System
description: Persistent file-based memory that survives across sessions. Store user preferences, project context, and feedback in ~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/.

title: GitHub CLI (gh)
description: Command-line tool for GitHub operations: create PRs, review code, manage issues, run actions. Claude Code uses it for all GitHub operations.

title: Plan Mode Pattern
description: Enter Plan Mode (Ctrl+G or Shift+Tab) before making changes. Claude explores and plans without modifying files, then you approve the plan before implementation. Reduces rework by 40-60%.

title: Verification Loop Pattern
description: After implementing changes, always verify: run tests, check lint, verify expected output. If verification fails, fix before moving on. Never assume the code works without evidence.

title: Progressive Context Loading
description: Start with minimal context (just filenames), then read specific sections as needed. This preserves context window for actual work instead of loading entire files you may not need.
